reasoning: The current absorbance seems unusually high given the values of molar absorptivity, concentration, and path length. Increasing the path length will help determine if it significantly affects the absorbance.

Doubling the path length doubled the absorbance, suggesting a linear relationship between absorbance and path length. To confirm the relationship between absorbance, molar absorptivity, concentration, and path length, I will now decrease the path length.

The absorbance returned to its original value after decreasing the path length, confirming the linear relationship. Given the consistent results across changes in path length, we can infer the Beer-Lambert law applies here. Now, I will check the effect of changing concentration to confirm the full equation.
The absorbance decreased proportionally with the decrease in concentration, confirming the relationship. With all factors confirmed, the final equation can be stated.
